name: Kurdan's Imperial Hunter
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wear leveled light armor including cuirass, greaves, and boots of the best quality available, with some wearing gauntlets and occasionally helmets. Beneath their armor they wear lower-class shoes and trousers. Armed with leveled shortswords of high quality.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: bloodthirsty, ruthless, sadistic, wealthy, thrill-seeking, merciless
Role: Wealthy clients who have paid Kurdan gro-Dragol substantial sums of gold for the privilege of participating in what he calls "the Hunter's Run" - the opportunity to hunt and kill human prey in Fort Grief. They represent the darkest kind of sport hunter, seeking thrills that can only come from stalking humanoid victims. They wait in the depths of Fort Grief for their prey to arrive, treating murder as entertainment.
Home Location Information: Fort Grief, specifically within the area known as the Hunter's Run - a dungeon designed for their deadly sport.
Daily Routine: The hunters do not sleep or eat. They spend all their time prowling their designated areas of the dungeon, waiting with anticipation for their prey to arrive so the hunt can begin.
The Hunter's Run: Kurdan gro-Dragol has created a profitable business by offering the ultimate hunting experience to those with fat purses and hearts lusting for blood. His clients pay him great deals of money to hunt living human prey with no questions asked, and Kurdan handles disposal of the bodies. The only way to escape Fort Grief once trapped inside is to descend into the Hunter's Run and kill the hunters - one of them carries the key to freedom. This is Kurdan's rule, and the only way victims can win. The hunters themselves are fully committed to this arrangement and will fight to the death, refusing to yield.
Personality - The Depraved Sportsmen: These are not ordinary hunters or even common criminals - they are wealthy individuals who have become so jaded and twisted that only the thrill of hunting human beings satisfies them. They have the means to indulge their darkest impulses and have found in Kurdan someone willing to facilitate their depravity. They show no mercy and expect none in return, viewing their victims as nothing more than game animals. Their willingness to pay substantial sums for this experience reveals the depth of their moral corruption.
author: Bethesda
